359.reason: [When a single '|' is encountered, this line incorrectly adds an OR token for "||" even though only one '|' was present. It should only add the token when both '|' characters are found.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, this line incorrectly adds an OR token for "||" even though only one '|' was present. It should only add the token when both '|' characters are found.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, it should not add an OR token "||", but the code always adds it regardless of whether the second '|' was found]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, an error is correctly reported, but the code still adds an "||" token regardless of whether the second '|' was actually present. This produces an incorrect token for invalid input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, an error is added but the code still creates an "||" token instead of handling the single '|' as invalid without producing a valid OR token]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [When a single '|' is encountered, an error is correctly reported but the code still adds an "||" token, which is wrong. It should only add the token when '||' is actually present.]

367.reason: [When a single '&' is encountered, this line incorrectly adds an AND token for "&&" even though only one '&' was present. It should only add the token when both '&' characters are found.]
